disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Normal distal power and sensation (grip, legs/feet), no bladder/bowel dysfunction (against cervical myelopathy/cord compression)
trigger_question_examples: Is your grip and leg/foot strength normal, and is sensation normal?
Any bladder or bowel changes, or numbness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My grip and legs feel strong and normal, sensation is normal, and no bladder/bowel changes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No temporal headache, scalp tenderness, jaw claudication, or visual changes (against a blood-vessel inflammation condition)
trigger_question_examples: Any headache over your temples, scalp tenderness, jaw ache when chewing, or visual changes?
Any blurring or loss of vision?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No headache, no scalp tenderness, no jaw pain when chewing, and no visual changes.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bilateral symmetric proximal (shoulder/hip girdle) stiffness, worse with rest/cold, no trauma
trigger_question_examples: Is the stiffness in both shoulders and hips equally, and worse after resting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Both shoulders and hips equally, worse after resting in the cold, and no fall or injury.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this a spinal cord problem? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen distal neurology (myelopathy) and the blood-vessel inflammation condition features; if the pattern is symmetric proximal with intact distal neurology and no the blood-vessel inflammation condition features, reassure a the inflammatory muscle-stiffness condition flare, give interim analgesia and warmth, arrange urgent next-day GP review to start low-dose steroids, and give a firm the blood-vessel inflammation condition safety-net — without ambulance over-triage.
